nodes.x: My soul is weary of my life; I will leave my complaint upon myself; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
For I have satiated the weary soul, and I have replenished every sorrowful soul.
Therefore I will not refrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.

My soul hath been redeemed from the gall of bitterness and bonds of iniquity. I was in the darkest abyss; but now I behold the marvelous light of God. My soul was racked with eternal torment; but I am snatched, and my soul is pained no more.
Sorrow and sighing flee away ; the bitter is sweet ; the afflicted glory in salvation and are upheld.
For I perceive that thou art in the gall of bitterness, and in the bond of iniquity.
I will say unto God, Do not condemn me; shew me wherefore thou contendest with me.

Therefore I say unto you, Go; and whosoever transgresseth against me, him shall ye judge according to the sins which he has committed; and if he confess his sins before thee and me, and repenteth in the sincerity of his heart, him shall ye forgive, and I will forgive him also.
And again I say unto you, if ye observe to do whatsoever I command you, I, the Lord, will turn away all wrath and indignation from you, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against you.
Therefore, keep these sayings which I have commanded you that ye come not under condemnation; for wo unto him whom the Father condemneth.
And if thou deal thus with me, kill me, I pray thee, out of hand, if I have found favour in thy sight; and let me not see my wretchedness.
O my people, what have I done unto thee? and wherein have I wearied thee? testify against me.
Is it good unto thee that thou shouldest oppress, that thou shouldest despise the work of thine hands, and shine upon the counsel of the wicked?
Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.
And now, behold, we are in thine hand: as it seemeth good and right unto thee to do unto us, do.
And if thy hand offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
For thou shalt eat the labour of thine hands: happy shalt thou be, and it shall be well with thee.
Suffer not thy mouth to cause thy flesh to sin; neither say thou before the angel, that it was an error: wherefore should God be angry at thy voice, and destroy the work of thine hands?
Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as man seeth?
The eye of him that hath seen me shall see me no more: thine eyes are upon me, and I am not.
And dost thou open thine eyes upon such an one, and bringest me into judgment with thee?

And the Lord said unto him: Because of thy faith thou hast seen that I shall take upon me flesh and blood; and never has man come before me with such exceeding faith as thou hast; for were it not so ye could not have seen my finger. Sawest thou more than this?
¶ Then said he unto me, Hast thou seen this, O son of man? turn thee yet again, and thou shalt see greater abominations than these.
Are thy days as the days of man? are thy years as man’s days,
For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased.
For all our days are passed away in thy wrath: we spend our years as a tale that is told.
I said, O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days: thy years are throughout all generations.
But thou art the same, and thy years shall have no end.
The days of our years are threescore years and ten; and if by reason of strength they be fourscore years, yet is their strength labour and sorrow; for it is soon cut off, and we fly away.
That thou inquirest after mine iniquity, and searchest after my sin?
If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.
For I will declare mine iniquity; I will be sorry for my sin.
That which I see not teach thou me: if I have done iniquity, I will do no more.
And why dost thou not pardon my transgression, and take away mine iniquity? for now shall I sleep in the dust; and thou shalt seek me in the morning, but I shall not be.
I acknowledged my sin unto thee, and mine iniquity have I not hid. I said, I will confess my transgressions unto the Lord; and thou forgavest the iniquity of my sin. Selah.
Thou knowest that I am not wicked; and there is none that can deliver out of thine hand.
As saith the proverb of the ancients, Wickedness proceedeth from the wicked: but mine hand shall not be upon thee.
For my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
Break thou the arm of the wicked and the evil man: seek out his wickedness till thou find none.
Woe unto the wicked! it shall be ill with him: for the reward of his hands shall be given him.
A froward heart shall depart from me: I will not know a wicked person.
Thine hands have made me and fashioned me together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.
Thou hast beset me behind and before, and laid thine hand upon me.
But put forth thine hand now, and touch all that he hath, and he will curse thee to thy face.
Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.
Thy hands have made me and fashioned me: give me understanding, that I may learn thy commandments.
Remember, I beseech thee, that thou hast made me as the clay; and wilt thou bring me into dust again?
The Lord shall make the rain of thy land powder and dust: from heaven shall it come down upon thee, until thou be destroyed.
In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken: for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.
But now, O Lord, thou art our father; we are the clay, and thou our potter; and we all are the work of thy hand.
And if thou wilt make me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stone: for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast polluted it.

And shake off the dust of thy feet against those who receive thee not, not in their presence, lest thou provoke them, but in secret; and wash thy feet, as a testimony against them in the day of judgment.
Hast thou not poured me out as milk, and curdled me like cheese?
For every one that useth milk is unskilful in the word of righteousness: for he is a babe.
I have fed you with milk, and not with meat: for hitherto ye were not able to bear it, neither yet now are ye able.
And he said unto me, Son of man, cause thy belly to eat, and fill thy bowels with this roll that I give thee. Then did I eat it; and it was in my mouth as honey for sweetness.
For I have eaten ashes like bread, and mingled my drink with weeping,
Thou shalt even drink it and suck it out, and thou shalt break the sherds thereof, and pluck off thine own breasts: for I have spoken it, saith the Lord God.
Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh, and hast fenced me with bones and sinews.
Thou hast given us like sheep appointed for meat; and hast scattered us among the heathen.
I clothed thee also with broidered work, and shod thee with badgers’ skin, and I girded thee about with fine linen, and I covered thee with silk.
Thou hast covered with anger, and persecuted us: thou hast slain, thou hast not pitied.
In one house shall it be eaten; thou shalt not carry forth ought of the flesh abroad out of the house; neither shall ye break a bone thereof.
Thou hast bought me no sweet cane with money, neither hast thou filled me with the fat of thy sacrifices: but thou hast made me to serve with thy sins, thou hast wearied me with thine iniquities.
Thou hast granted me life and favour, and thy visitation hath preserved my spirit.
Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy right hand hath holden me up, and thy gentleness hath made me great.
Thou shalt guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.
Let my soul live, and it shall praise thee; and let thy judgments help me.
Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy gentleness hath made me great.
And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.
And these things hast thou hid in thine heart: I know that this is with thee.
And these words, which I command thee this day, shall be in thine heart:
Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee.
Saying, If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things which belong unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes.
Murmur not because of the things which thou hast not seen, for they are withheld from thee and from the world, which is wisdom in me in a time to come.
Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Because thou hast set thine heart as the heart of God;
If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.
That thou inquirest after mine iniquity, and searchest after my sin?
Thou hast set our iniquities before thee, our secret sins in the light of thy countenance.
If I be wicked, woe unto me; and if I be righteous, yet will I not lift up my head. I am full of confusion; therefore see thou mine affliction;
Yea, wo be unto you because of your wickedness and abominations!
Again, when I say unto the wicked, Thou shalt surely die; if he turn from his sin, and do that which is lawful and right;

When I say unto the wicked, O wicked man, thou shalt surely die; if thou dost not speak to warn the wicked from his way, that wicked man shall die in his iniquity; but his blood will I require at thine hand.
Yet if thou warn the wicked, and he turn not from his wickedness, nor from his wicked way, he shall die in his iniquity; but thou hast delivered thy soul.
The way of the wicked is an abomination unto the Lord: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness.
For it increaseth. Thou huntest me as a fierce lion: and again thou shewest thyself marvellous upon me.

And although their influence shall cast thee into trouble, and into bars and walls, thou shalt be had in honor; and but for a small moment and thy voice shall be more terrible in the midst of thine enemies than the fierce lion, because of thy righteousness; and thy God shall stand by thee forever and ever.
Thou art more glorious and excellent than the mountains of prey.
Wilt thou hunt the prey for the lion? or fill the appetite of the young lions,
Lord, how long wilt thou look on? rescue my soul from their destructions, my darling from the lions.
Therefore I will be unto them as a lion: as a leopard by the way will I observe them:
Thou renewest thy witnesses against me, and increasest thine indignation upon me; changes and war are against me.
I say, sayest thou, (but they are but vain words) I have counsel and strength for war: now on whom dost thou trust, that thou rebellest against me?
Thou sayest, (but they are but vain words,) I have counsel and strength for the war. Now on whom dost thou trust, that thou rebellest against me?
These things hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether such an one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set them in order before thine eyes.
¶ Likewise, thou son of man, set thy face against the daughters of thy people, which prophesy out of their own heart; and prophesy thou against them,
Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb? Oh that I had given up the ghost, and no eye had seen me!
Why died I not from the womb? why did I not give up the ghost when I came out of the belly?
For thou hast possessed my reins: thou hast covered me in my mother’s womb.

And I looked and beheld the Redeemer of the world, of whom my father had spoken; and I also beheld the prophet who should prepare the way before him. And the Lamb of God went forth and was baptized of him; and after he was baptized, I beheld the heavens open, and the Holy Ghost come down out of heaven and abide upon him in the form of a dove.
Wherefore came I forth out of the womb to see labour and sorrow, that my days should be consumed with shame?
But thou art he that took me out of the womb: thou didst make me hope when I was upon my mother’s breasts.
I should have been as though I had not been; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.
Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb? Oh that I had given up the ghost, and no eye had seen me!

… I saw exactly what would come to pass if there was not something done. I have had this spirit upon me for a long time. But I want to say this: I should have let all the temples go out of our hands; I should have gone to prison myself, and let every other man go there, had not the God of heaven commanded me to do what I did do; and when the hour came that I was commanded to do that, it was all…
Because he slew me not from the womb; or that my mother might have been my grave, and her womb to be always great with me.
Or as an hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants which never saw light.
Are not my days few? cease then, and let me alone, that I may take comfort a little,

Mother, do you not know, that you are one of the most singular
    women in the world? "No," I replied, "I do not." Well I do, he
    continued, you have brought up my children for me by the fireside,
    and when I was gone from home, you comforted them. You have brought
    up all my children, and could always comfort them when I could
    not. We have often wished that we might both die at the…
Lord, make me to know mine end, and the measure of my days, what it is; that I may know how frail I am.
I am troubled; I am bowed down greatly; I go mourning all the day long.
Nevertheless in those days, saith the Lord, I will not make a full end with you.
Before I go whence I shall not return, even to the land of darkness and the shadow of death;
When a few years are come, then I shall go the way whence I shall not return.
A land of darkness, as darkness itself; and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness.
But to the land whereunto they desire to return, thither shall they not return.

am: for the vail of darkness ahall soon bo rent, and he that is not purified shall not abide the day; wherefore gird up your loins and be prepared. Behold the kingdom is yours and the enemy shall not overcome.
A land of darkness, as darkness itself; and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness.

2. The light and the life of the world; a light which shineth in
    darkness and the darkness comprehendeth it not;
The light and the life of the world; a light which shineth in darkness and the darkness comprehendeth it not;
The light and the life of the world, a light which shineth in darkness and the darkness comprehendeth it not;

Give glory to the Lord your God, before he cause darkness, and before your feet stumble upon the dark mountains, and, while ye look for light, he turn it into the shadow of death, and make it gross darkness.
